name: White Lime Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a medium saucepan.
2. Stir in sugar and milk until the sugar dissolves.
3. Bring to a boil. Cover and boil for 1 minute to dissolve any sugar that may be on the sides of the pan.
4. Insert a candy thermometer into the pan and cook gently without stirring until it reaches 236°F (soft-ball stage).
5. Allow the mixture to cool to 110°F to 120°F (warm) for about 45 minutes.
6. Stir in lime peel and pecans.
7. Beat vigorously until the mixture starts to lose its gloss.
8. Pour immediately into a greased 8x8x2-inch pan.
9. Cool, then cut into squares.
10. Store in plastic bags or an airtight container. Will keep about 3 weeks. Makes about 1 pound.
(Note: From Woman's Day, November 15, 1977)
